sentences.text: A method for recording control flow transfer history is valuable not only for performance profiling but also for debugging.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0

sentences.text: Control flow transfers refer to jump instructions (including function calls and returns), taken branch instructions, traps, and trap returns.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0

sentences.text: Profiling tools, such as Linux perf, collect control transfer history when sampling software execution, thereby enabling tools, like AutoFDO, to identify hot paths for optimization.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0

sentences.text: Control flow trace capabilities offer very deep transfer history, but the volume of data produced can result in significant performance overheads due to memory bandwidth consumption, buffer management, and decoder overhead.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0

sentences.text: The Control Transfer Records (CTR) extension provides a method to record a limited history in register-accessible internal chip storage, with the intent of dramatically reducing the performance overhead and complexity of collecting transfer history.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0

sentences.text: CTR defines a circular (FIFO) buffer.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0

sentences.text: Each buffer entry holds a record for a single recorded control flow transfer.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0

sentences.text: The number of records that can be held in the buffer depends upon both the implementation (the maximum supported depth) and the CTR configuration (the software selected depth).
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0

sentences.text: Only qualified transfers are recorded.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0

sentences.text: Qualified transfers are those that meet the filtering criteria, which include the privilege mode and the transfer type.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0

sentences.text: Recorded transfers are inserted at the write pointer, which is then incremented, while older recorded transfers may be overwritten once the buffer is full.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0

sentences.text: Or the user can enable RAS (Return Address Stack) emulation mode, where only function calls are recorded, and function returns pop the last call record.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0

sentences.text: The source PC, target PC, and some optional metadata (transfer type, elapsed cycles) are stored for each recorded transfer.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0

sentences.text: The CTR buffer is accessible through an indirect CSR interface, such that software can specify which logical entry in the buffer it wishes to read or write.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0

sentences.text: Logical entry 0 always corresponds to the youngest recorded transfer, followed by entry 1 as the next youngest, and so on.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0

sentences.text: The machine-level extension, *Smctr*, encompasses all newly added Control Status Registers (CSRs), instructions, and behavior modifications for a hart across all privilege levels.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0

sentences.text: The corresponding supervisor-level extension, *Ssctr*, is essentially identical to Smctr, except that it excludes machine-level CSRs and behaviors not intended to be directly accessible at the supervisor level.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0

sentences.text: Smctr and Ssctr depend on both the implementation of S-mode and the Sscsrind extension.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0

sentences.text: The `mctrctl` register is a 64-bit read/write register that enables and configures the CTR capability.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Machine Control Transfer Records Control Register (`mctrctl`)

sentences.text: The depth of the CTR buffer dictates the number of entries to which the hardware records transfers.

sentences.text: For a depth of N, the hardware records transfers to entries 0..N-1.

sentences.text: All read as '0' and are read-only when the selected entry is in the range N to 255.

sentences.text: When the depth is increased, the newly accessible entries contain unspecified but legal values.

sentences.text: Attempts to access `sctrdepth` from VS-mode or VU-mode raise a virtual-instruction exception, unless CTR state enable access restrictions apply.

sentences.text: [NOTE] It is expected that operating systems (OSs) will access `sctrdepth` only at boot, to select the maximum supported depth value.

sentences.text: More frequent accesses may result in reduced performance in virtualization scenarios, as a result of traps from VS-mode incurred.

sentences.text: There may be scenarios where software chooses to operate on only a subset of the entries, to reduce overhead.

sentences.text: In such cases tools may choose to read only the lower entries, and OSs may choose to save/restore only on the lower entries while using SCTRCLR to clear the others.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Machine Control Transfer Records Control Register (`mctrctl`) > Supervisor Control Transfer Records Depth Register (`sctrdepth`)

sentences.text: The value in configurable depth lies in supporting VM migration.

sentences.text: It is expected that a platform spec may specify that one or more CTR depth values must be supported. A hypervisor may wish to restrict guests to using one of these required depths, in order to ensure that such guests can be migrated to any system that complies with the platform spec.

sentences.text: The trapping behavior specified for VS-mode accesses to `sctrdepth` ensures that the hypervisor can impose such restrictions.

sentences.text: The 32-bit `sctrstatus` register grants access to CTR status information and is updated by the hardware whenever CTR is active. CTR is active when the current privilege mode is enabled for recording and CTR is not frozen.

sentences.text: This ensures that the software writes to logical CTR entries modify the proper physical entries.

sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Machine Control Transfer Records Control Register (`mctrctl`) > Supervisor Control Transfer Records Status Register (`sctrstatus`)

sentences.text: If a qualified control transfer is emulated, the emulator can simply increment the WRPTR, then write the synthesized record to logical entry 0.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Machine Control Transfer Records Control Register (`mctrctl`) > Supervisor Control Transfer Records Status Register (`sctrstatus`)

sentences.text: If a qualified function return is emulated while RASEMU=1, the emulator can clear `ctrsource`.V for logical entry 0, then decrement the WRPTR.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Machine Control Transfer Records Control Register (`mctrctl`) > Supervisor Control Transfer Records Status Register (`sctrstatus`)

sentences.text: Exposing the WRPTR may also allow support for Linux perf's https://lwn.net/Articles/802821[[.underline]stack stitching] capability.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Machine Control Transfer Records Control Register (`mctrctl`) > Supervisor Control Transfer Records Status Register (`sctrstatus`)

sentences.text: [NOTE] Smctr/Ssctr depends upon implementation of S-mode because much of CTR state is accessible only through S-mode CSRs.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Machine Control Transfer Records Control Register (`mctrctl`) > Supervisor Control Transfer Records Status Register (`sctrstatus`)

sentences.text: If, in the future, it becomes desirable to remove this dependency, an extension could add `mctrdepth` and `mctrstatus` CSRs that reflect the same state as `sctrdepth` and `sctrstatus`, respectively.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Machine Control Transfer Records Control Register (`mctrctl`) > Supervisor Control Transfer Records Status Register (`sctrstatus`)

sentences.text: Further, such an extension should make CTR entries accessible via `miselect`/`mireg*`.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Machine Control Transfer Records Control Register (`mctrctl`) > Supervisor Control Transfer Records Status Register (`sctrstatus`)

sentences.text: Control transfer records are stored in a CTR buffer, such that each buffer entry stores information about a single transfer.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Entry Registers

sentences.text: The CTR buffer entries are logically accessed via the indirect register access mechanism defined by the Sscsrind extension.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Entry Registers

sentences.text: The `siselect` index range 0x200 through 0x2FF is reserved for CTR logical entries 0 through 255.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Entry Registers

sentences.text: When `siselect` holds a value in this range, `sireg` provides access to `ctrsource`, `sireg2` provides access to `ctrtarget`, and `sireg3` provides access to `ctrdata`. `sireg4`, `sireg5`, and `sireg6` are read-only 0.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Entry Registers

sentences.text: When `vsiselect` holds a value in 0x200..0x2FF, the `vsireg*` registers provide access to the same CTR entry register state as the analogous `sireg*` registers.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Entry Registers

sentences.text: There is not a separate set of entry registers for V=1.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Entry Registers

sentences.text: See for cases where CTR accesses from S-mode and VS-mode may be restricted.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Entry Registers

sentences.text: The `ctrsource` register contains the source program counter, which is the `pc` of the recorded control transfer instruction, or the epc of the recorded trap.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Entry Registers > Control Transfer Record Source Register (`ctrsource`)

sentences.text: The valid (V) bit is set by the hardware when a transfer is recorded in the selected CTR buffer entry, and implies that data in `ctrsource`, `ctrtarget`, and `ctrdata` is valid for this entry.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Entry Registers > Control Transfer Record Source Register (`ctrsource`)

sentences.text: `ctrsource` is an MXLEN-bit WARL register that must be able to hold all valid virtual or physical addresses that can serve as a `pc`.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Entry Registers > Control Transfer Record Source Register (`ctrsource`)

sentences.text: It need not be able to hold any invalid addresses; implementations may convert an invalid address into a valid address that the register is capable of holding.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Entry Registers > Control Transfer Record Source Register (`ctrsource`)

sentences.text: When XLEN < MXLEN, both explicit writes (by software) and implicit writes (for recorded transfers) will be zero-extended.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Entry Registers > Control Transfer Record Source Register (`ctrsource`)

sentences.text: .Control Transfer Record Source Register Format for MXLEN=64 [wavedrom, , svg] .... {reg: [ {bits: 1, name: 'V'}, {bits: 63, name: 'PC[63:1]'}, ], config:{lanes: 1, hspace: 1024}} ....
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Entry Registers > Control Transfer Record Source Register (`ctrsource`)

sentences.text: [NOTE] CTR entry registers are defined as MXLEN, despite the `xireg*` CSRs used to access them being XLEN, to ensure that entries recorded in RV64 are not truncated, as a result of CSR Width Modulation, on a transition to RV32.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Entry Registers > Control Transfer Record Source Register (`ctrsource`)

sentences.text: The `ctrtarget` register contains the target (destination) program counter of the recorded transfer.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Entry Registers > Control Transfer Record Target Register (`ctrtarget`)

sentences.text: For a not-taken branch, `ctrtarget` holds the PC of the next sequential instruction following the branch.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Entry Registers > Control Transfer Record Target Register (`ctrtarget`)

sentences.text: The optional MISP bit is set by the hardware when the recorded transfer is an instruction whose target or taken/not-taken direction was mispredicted by the branch predictor. MISP is read-only 0 when not implemented.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Entry Registers > Control Transfer Record Target Register (`ctrtarget`)

sentences.text: `ctrtarget` is an MXLEN-bit WARL register that must be able to hold all valid virtual or physical addresses that can serve as a `pc`.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Entry Registers > Control Transfer Record Target Register (`ctrtarget`)

sentences.text: It need not be able to hold any invalid addresses; implementations may convert an invalid address into a valid address that the register is capable of holding.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Entry Registers > Control Transfer Record Target Register (`ctrtarget`)

sentences.text: When XLEN < MXLEN, both explicit writes (by software) and implicit writes (by recorded transfers) will be zero-extended.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Entry Registers > Control Transfer Record Target Register (`ctrtarget`)

sentences.text: .Control Transfer Record Target Register Format for MXLEN=64 [wavedrom, , svg] .... {reg: [ {bits: 1, name: 'MISP'}, {bits: 31, name: 'PC[31:1]'}, {bits: 32, name: 'PC[63:32]'}, ], config:{lanes: 2, hspace: 1024}} ....
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Entry Registers > Control Transfer Record Target Register (`ctrtarget`)

sentences.text: The `ctrdata` register contains metadata for the recorded transfer.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Entry Registers > Control Transfer Record Metadata Register (`ctrdata`)

sentences.text: This register must be implemented, though all fields within it are optional.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Entry Registers > Control Transfer Record Metadata Register (`ctrdata`)

sentences.text: Unimplemented fields are read-only 0. `ctrdata` is a 64-bit register.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Entry Registers > Control Transfer Record Metadata Register (`ctrdata`)

sentences.text: .Control Transfer Record Metadata Register Format [wavedrom, , svg] .... {reg: [ {bits: 4, name: 'TYPE'}, {bits: 11, name: 'WPRI'}, {bits: 1, name: 'CCV'}, {bits: 16, name: 'CC'}, {bits: 32, name: 'WPRI'}, ], config:{lanes: 2, hspace: 1024}} ....
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Entry Registers > Control Transfer Record Metadata Register (`ctrdata`)

sentences.text: .Control Transfer Record Metadata Register Field Definitions [width="100%",cols="15%,75%,10%",options="header",] | |Field |Description |Access |TYPE[3:0] a| Identifies the type of the control flow transfer recorded in the entry, using the encodings listed in xref:transfer-type-defs[xrefstyle=short].
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Entry Registers > Control Transfer Record Metadata Register (`ctrdata`)

sentences.text: Implementations that do not support this field will report 0. |WARL
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Entry Registers > Control Transfer Record Metadata Register (`ctrdata`)

sentences.text: |CC[15:0] |Cycle Count, composed of the Cycle Count Exponent (CCE, in CC[15:12]) and Cycle Count Mantissa (CCM, in CC[11:0]).
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Entry Registers > Control Transfer Record Metadata Register (`ctrdata`)

sentences.text: Undefined bits in `ctrdata` are WPRI.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Entry Registers > Control Transfer Record Metadata Register (`ctrdata`)

sentences.text: Undefined bits must be implemented as read-only 0, unless a is implemented and enabled.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Entry Registers > Control Transfer Record Metadata Register (`ctrdata`)

sentences.text: [NOTE] Like the bits in `mctrctl`, the `ctrdata`.TYPE bits leverage the E-trace itype encodings.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Entry Registers > Control Transfer Record Metadata Register (`ctrdata`)

sentences.text: [wavedrom, ,svg] .... {reg: [ {bits: 7, name: 'opcode', attr: ['7', 'SYSTEM'], type: 8}, {bits: 5, name: 'rd', attr: ['5', '0'], type: 2}, {bits: 3, name: 'funct3', attr: ['3', '0'], type: 8}, {bits: 5, name: 'rs1', attr: ['5', '0'], type: 4}, {bits: 12, name: 'func12', attr: ['12', 'SCTRCLR (0x104)'], type: 8}, ]} ....
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Instructions > Supervisor CTR Clear Instruction

sentences.text: The SCTRCLR instruction performs the following operations:
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Instructions > Supervisor CTR Clear Instruction

sentences.text: Zeroes all CTR , for all DEPTH values * Zeroes the CTR cycle counter and CCV (see )
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Instructions > Supervisor CTR Clear Instruction

sentences.text: Any read of `ctrsource`, `ctrtarget`, or `ctrdata` that follows SCTRCLR, such that it precedes the next qualified control transfer, will return the value 0.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Instructions > Supervisor CTR Clear Instruction

sentences.text: Further, the first recorded transfer following SCTRCLR will have `ctrdata`.CCV=0.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Instructions > Supervisor CTR Clear Instruction

sentences.text: SCTRCLR raises an illegal-instruction exception in U-mode, and a virtual-instruction exception in VU-mode, unless CTR state enable access restrictions apply.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Instructions > Supervisor CTR Clear Instruction

sentences.text: When Smstateen is implemented, the `mstateen0`.CTR bit controls access to CTR register state from privilege modes less privileged than M-mode.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > State Enable Access Control

sentences.text: When `mstateen0`.CTR=1, accesses to CTR register state behave as described in and above, while SCTRCLR behaves as described in .
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > State Enable Access Control

sentences.text: When `mstateen0`.CTR=0 and the privilege mode is less privileged than M-mode, the following operations raise an illegal-instruction exception:
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > State Enable Access Control

sentences.text: Attempts to access `sctrctl`, `vsctrctl`, `sctrdepth`, or `sctrstatus` * Attempts to access `sireg*` when `siselect` is in 0x200..0x2FF, or `vsireg*` when `vsiselect` is in 0x200..0x2FF * Execution of the SCTRCLR instruction
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > State Enable Access Control

sentences.text: When `mstateen0`.CTR=0, qualified control transfers executed in privilege modes less privileged than M-mode will continue to implicitly update entry registers and `sctrstatus`.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > State Enable Access Control

sentences.text: If the H extension is implemented and `mstateen0`.CTR=1, the `hstateen0`.CTR bit controls access to supervisor CTR state when V=1.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > State Enable Access Control

sentences.text: This state includes `sctrctl` (really `vsctrctl`), `sctrstatus`, and `sireg*` (really `vsireg*`) when `siselect` (really `vsiselect`) is in 0x200..0x2FF. `hstateen0`.CTR is read-only 0 when `mstateen0`.CTR=0.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > State Enable Access Control

sentences.text: When `mstateen0`.CTR=1 and `hstateen0`.CTR=1, VS-mode accesses to supervisor CTR state behave as described in and above, while SCTRCLR behaves as described in .
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > State Enable Access Control

sentences.text: When `mstateen0`.CTR=1 and `hstateen0`.CTR=0, both VS-mode accesses to supervisor CTR state and VS-mode execution of SCTRCLR raise a virtual-instruction exception.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > State Enable Access Control

sentences.text: [NOTE] `sctrdepth` is not included in the above list of supervisor CTR state controlled by `hstateen0`.CTR since accesses to `sctrdepth` from VS-mode raise a virtual-instruction exception regardless of the value of `hstateen0`.CTR.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > State Enable Access Control

sentences.text: When `hstateen0`.CTR=0, qualified control transfers executed while V=1 will continue to implicitly update entry registers and `sctrstatus`.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > State Enable Access Control

sentences.text: [NOTE] See for how bit 60 in `mstateen0` and `hstateen0` can also restrict access to `sireg*`/`siselect` and `vsireg*`/`vsiselect` from privilege modes less privileged than M-mode.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > State Enable Access Control

sentences.text: [NOTE] Implementations that support Smctr/Ssctr but not Smstateen/Ssstateen may observe reduced performance.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > State Enable Access Control

sentences.text: Because Smctr/Ssctr introduces a significant number of new CSRs, it is desirable to avoid save/restore of CTR state when possible. A hypervisor is likely to leverage State Enable to trap on the initial guest access to CTR state, delegating CTR and enabling save/restore of guest CTR state only once the guest has begun to use it.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > State Enable Access Control

sentences.text: Without Smstateen/Ssstateen, a hypervisor is required to save/restore guest CTR state on every context switch.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > State Enable Access Control

sentences.text: CTR records qualified control transfers.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior

sentences.text: Control transfers are qualified if they meet the following criteria:
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior

sentences.text: The current privilege mode is enabled * The transfer type is not inhibited * `sctrstatus`.FROZEN is not set * The transfer completes/retires
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior

sentences.text: Such qualified transfers update the at logical entry 0.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior

sentences.text: As a result, older entries are pushed down the stack; the record previously in logical entry 0 moves to logical entry 1, the record in logical entry 1 moves to logical entry 2, and so on.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior

sentences.text: If the CTR buffer is full, the oldest recorded entry (previously at entry depth-1) is lost.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior

sentences.text: Recorded transfers will set the `ctrsource`.V bit to 1, and will update all implemented record fields.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior

sentences.text: [NOTE] In order to collect accurate and representative performance profiles while using CTR, it is recommended that hardware recording of control transfers incurs no added performance overhead, e.g., in the form of retirement or instruction execution restrictions that are not present when CTR is not active.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior

sentences.text: Transfers that change the privilege mode are a special case.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Privilege Mode Transitions

sentences.text: What is recorded, if anything, depends on whether the source privilege mode and/or target privilege mode are enabled for recording, and on the transfer type (trap or trap return).
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Privilege Mode Transitions

sentences.text: Traps between enabled privilege modes are recorded as normal.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Privilege Mode Transitions

sentences.text: Traps from a disabled privilege mode to an enabled privilege mode are partially recorded, such that the `ctrsource`.PC is 0.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Privilege Mode Transitions

sentences.text: Traps from an enabled mode to a disabled mode, known as external traps, are not recorded by default.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Privilege Mode Transitions

sentences.text: See for how they can be recorded.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Privilege Mode Transitions

sentences.text: Trap returns have similar treatment.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Privilege Mode Transitions

sentences.text: Trap returns between enabled privilege modes are recorded as normal.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Privilege Mode Transitions

sentences.text: Trap returns from an enabled mode back to a disabled mode are partially recorded, such that `ctrtarget`.PC is 0.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Privilege Mode Transitions

sentences.text: Trap returns from a disabled mode to an enabled mode are not recorded.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Privilege Mode Transitions

sentences.text: Recording in Debug Mode is always inhibited.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Privilege Mode Transitions

sentences.text: Transfers into and out of Debug Mode are never recorded.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Privilege Mode Transitions

sentences.text: The table below provides details on recording of privilege mode transitions.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Privilege Mode Transitions

sentences.text: Standard dependencies on FROZEN and transfer type inhibits also apply, but are not covered by the table.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Privilege Mode Transitions

sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Privilege Mode Transitions

sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Privilege Mode Transitions

sentences.text: Transitions between VS/VU-mode and M/HS-mode are unique in that they effect a change in the active CTR control register, and hence the CTR configuration.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Privilege Mode Transitions > Virtualization Mode Transitions

sentences.text: What is recorded, if anything, on these virtualization mode transitions depends upon fields from both `[ms]ctrctl` and `vsctrctl`.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Privilege Mode Transitions > Virtualization Mode Transitions

sentences.text: `mctrctl`.M, `sctrctl`.S, and `vsctrctl`.{S,U} are used to determine whether the source and target modes are enabled; * `mctrctl`.MTE, `sctrctl`.STE, and `vsctrctl`.STE are used to determine whether an external trap is recorded (see ); * `sctrctl`.LCOFIFRZ and `sctrctl`.BPFRZ determine whether CTR becomes frozen (see ) * For all other `xctrctl` fields, the value in `vsctrctl` is used.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Privilege Mode Transitions > Virtualization Mode Transitions

sentences.text: External traps are traps from a privilege mode enabled for CTR recording to a privilege mode that is not enabled for CTR recording.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Privilege Mode Transitions > External Traps

sentences.text: By default external traps are not recorded, but privileged software running in the target mode of the trap can opt-in to allowing CTR to record external traps into that mode.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Privilege Mode Transitions > External Traps

sentences.text: The `xctrctl`.xTE bits allow M-mode, S-mode, and VS-mode to opt-in separately.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Privilege Mode Transitions > External Traps

sentences.text: External trap recording depends not only on the target mode, but on any intervening modes, which are modes that are more privileged than the source mode but less privileged than the target mode.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Privilege Mode Transitions > External Traps

sentences.text: Not only must the external trap enable bit for the target mode be set, but the external trap enable bit(s) for any intervening modes must also be set.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Privilege Mode Transitions > External Traps

sentences.text: See the table below for details.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Privilege Mode Transitions > External Traps

sentences.text: [NOTE] Requiring intervening modes to be enabled for external traps simplifies software management of CTR.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Privilege Mode Transitions > External Traps

sentences.text: Consider a scenario where S-mode software is configuring CTR for U-mode contexts A and B, such that external traps (to any mode) are enabled for A but not for B.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Privilege Mode Transitions > External Traps

sentences.text: When switching between the two contexts, S-mode can simply toggle `sctrctl`.STE, rather than requiring a trap to M-mode to additionally toggle `mctrctl`.MTE.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Privilege Mode Transitions > External Traps

sentences.text: This method does not provide the flexibility to record external traps to a more privileged mode but not to all intervening mode(s).
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Privilege Mode Transitions > External Traps

sentences.text: Because it is expected that profiling tools generally wish to observe all external traps or none, this is not considered a meaningful limitation.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Privilege Mode Transitions > External Traps

sentences.text: .External Trap Enable Requirements [options="header", width="85%", cols="23%,23%,54%"] | |Source Mode |Target Mode |External Trap Enable(s) Required .2+|U-mode | S-mode | `sctrctl`.STE |M-mode | `mctrctl`.MTE, `sctrctl`.STE |S-mode | M-mode | `mctrctl`.MTE .3+|VU-mode | VS-mode | `vsctrctl`.STE | HS-mode | `sctrctl`.STE, `vsctrctl`.STE | M-mode | `mctrctl`.MTE, `sctrctl`.STE, `vsctrctl`.STE .2+| VS-mode | HS-mode | `sctrctl`.STE | M-mode | `mctrctl`.MTE, `sctrctl`.STE |
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Privilege Mode Transitions > External Traps

sentences.text: In records for external traps, the `ctrtarget`.PC is 0.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Privilege Mode Transitions > External Traps

sentences.text: [NOTE] No mechanism exists for recording external trap returns, because the external trap record includes all relevant information, and gives the trap handler (e.g., an emulator) the opportunity to modify the record.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Privilege Mode Transitions > External Traps

sentences.text: [NOTE] Note that external trap recording does not depend on EXCINH/INTRINH.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Privilege Mode Transitions > External Traps

sentences.text: Thus, when external traps are enabled, both external interrupts and external exceptions are recorded.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Privilege Mode Transitions > External Traps

sentences.text: STE allows recording of traps from U-mode to S-mode as well as from VS/VU-mode to HS-mode.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Privilege Mode Transitions > External Traps

sentences.text: The hypervisor can flip `sctrctl`.STE before entering a guest if it wants different behavior for U-to-S vs VS/VU-to-HS.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Privilege Mode Transitions > External Traps

sentences.text: If external trap recording is implemented, `mctrctl`.MTE and `sctrctl`.STE must be implemented, while `vsctrctl`.STE must be implemented if the H extension is implemented.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Privilege Mode Transitions > External Traps

sentences.text: Default CTR behavior, when all transfer type filter bits (`xctrctl`[47:32]) are unimplemented or 0, is to record all control transfers within enabled privileged modes.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Transfer Type Filtering

sentences.text: By setting transfer type filter bits, software can opt out of recording select transfer types, or opt into recording non-default operations.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Transfer Type Filtering

sentences.text: All transfer type filter bits are optional.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Transfer Type Filtering

sentences.text: [NOTE] Because not-taken branches are not recorded by default, the polarity of the associated enable bit (NTBREN) is the opposite of other bits associated with transfer type filtering (TKBRINH, RETINH, etc).
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Transfer Type Filtering

sentences.text: Non-default operations require opt-in rather than opt-out.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Transfer Type Filtering

sentences.text: The transfer type filter bits leverage the type definitions specified in the https://github.com/riscv-non-isa/riscv-trace-spec/releases/download/v2.0rc2/riscv-trace-spec.pdf[[.underline]RISC-V Efficient Trace Spec v2.0] (Table 4.4 and Section 4.1.1).
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Transfer Type Filtering

sentences.text: For completeness, the definitions are reproduced below.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Transfer Type Filtering

sentences.text: .Control Transfer Type Definitions [transfer-type-defs] [width="60%", cols="22%,78%", options="header",] | | Encoding | Transfer Type Name | 0 | Not used by CTR | 1 | Exception | 2 | Interrupt | 3 | Trap return | 4 | Not-taken branch | 5 | Taken branch | 6 | reserved | 7 | reserved | 8 | Indirect call | 9 | Direct call | 10 | Indirect jump (without linkage) | 11 | Direct jump (without linkage) | 12 | Co-routine swap | 13 | Function return | 14 | Other indirect jump (with linkage) | 15 | Other direct jump (with linkage) |
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Transfer Type Filtering

sentences.text: Encodings 8 through 15 refer to various encodings of jump instructions.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Transfer Type Filtering

sentences.text: The types are distinguished as described below.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Transfer Type Filtering

sentences.text: .Control Transfer Type Definitions [cols="37%,63%", options="header",] | | Transfer Type Name | Associated Opcodes .3+| Indirect call | JALR x1, rs where rs != x5 | JALR x5, rs where rs != x1 | C.JALR rs1 where rs1 != x5 .4+| Direct call | JAL x1 | JAL x5 | C.JAL | CM.JALT index .2+| Indirect jump (without linkage) | JALR x0, rs where rs != (x1 or x5) | C.JR rs1 where rs1 != (x1 or x5) .3+| Direct jump (without linkage) | JAL x0 | C.J | CM.JT index .3+| Co-routine swap | JALR x1, x5 | JALR x5, x1 | C.JALR x5 .3+| Function return | JALR rd, rs where rs (x1 or x5) and rd != (x1 or x5) | C.JR rs1 where rs1 (x1 or x5) | CM.POPRET(Z) | Other indirect jump (with linkage) | JALR rd, rs where rs != (x1 or x5) and rd != (x0, x1, or x5) | Other direct jump (with linkage) | JAL rd where rd != (x0, x1, or x5) |
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Transfer Type Filtering

sentences.text: If implementation of any transfer type filter bit results in reduced software performance, perhaps due to additional retirement restrictions, it is strongly recommended that this reduced performance apply only when the bit is set.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Transfer Type Filtering

sentences.text: Alternatively, support for the bit may be omitted.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Transfer Type Filtering

sentences.text: Maintaining software performance for the default CTR configuration, when all transfer type bits are cleared, is recommended.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Transfer Type Filtering

sentences.text: The `ctrdata` register may optionally include a count of CPU cycles elapsed since the prior CTR record.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Cycle Counting

sentences.text: The elapsed cycle count value is represented by the CC field, which has a 12-bit mantissa component (Cycle Count Mantissa, or CCM) and a 4-bit exponent component (Cycle Count Exponent, or CCE).
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Cycle Counting

sentences.text: The elapsed cycle counter (CtrCycleCounter) increments at the same rate as the `mcycle` counter.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Cycle Counting

sentences.text: Only cycles while CTR is active are counted, where active implies that the current privilege mode is enabled for recording and CTR is not frozen.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Cycle Counting

sentences.text: The CC field is encoded such that CCE holds 0 if the CtrCycleCounter value is less than 4096, otherwise it holds the index of the most significant one bit in the CtrCycleCounter value, minus 11. CCM holds CtrCycleCounter bits CCE+10:CCE-1.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Cycle Counting

sentences.text: The elapsed cycle count can then be calculated by software using the following formula:
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Cycle Counting

sentences.text: [subs="specialchars,quotes"] ---- if (CCE0): return CCM else: return (2^12^ + CCM) << CCE-1 endif ----
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Cycle Counting

sentences.text: The CtrCycleCounter is reset on writes to `xctrctl`, and on execution of SCTRCLR, to ensure that any accumulated cycle counts do not persist across a context switch.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Cycle Counting

sentences.text: An implementation that supports cycle counting must implement CCV and all CCM bits, but may implement 0..4 exponent bits in CCE.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Cycle Counting

sentences.text: Unimplemented CCE bits are read-only 0.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Cycle Counting

sentences.text: For implementations that support transfer type filtering, it is recommended to implement at least 3 exponent bits.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Cycle Counting

sentences.text: This allows capturing the full latency of most functions, when recording only calls and returns.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Cycle Counting

sentences.text: The size of the CtrCycleCounter required to support each CCE width is given in the table below.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Cycle Counting

sentences.text: .Cycle Counter Size Options [width="70%", cols="20%,38%,42%", options="header",] | | CCE bits | CtrCycleCounter bits | Max elapsed cycle value | 0 | 12 | 4095 | 1 | 13 | 8191 | 2 | 15 | 32764 | 3 | 19 | 524224 | 4 | 27 | 134201344 |
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Cycle Counting

sentences.text: When CCE>1, the granularity of the reported cycle count is reduced.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Cycle Counting

sentences.text: For example, when CCE=3, the bottom 2 bits of the cycle counter are not reported, and thus the reported value increments only every 4 cycles.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Cycle Counting

sentences.text: As a result, the reported value represents an undercount of elapsed cycles for most cases (when the unreported bits are non-zero).
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Cycle Counting

sentences.text: On average, the undercount will be (2^CCE-1^-1)/2.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Cycle Counting

sentences.text: Software can reduce the average undercount to 0 by adding (2^CCE-1^-1)/2 to each computed cycle count value when CCE>1.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Cycle Counting

sentences.text: Though this compressed method of representation results in some imprecision for larger cycle count values, it produces meaningful area savings, reducing storage per entry from 27 bits to 16.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Cycle Counting

sentences.text: The CC value saturates when all implemented bits in CCM and CCE are 1.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Cycle Counting

sentences.text: The CC value is valid only when the Cycle Count Valid (CCV) bit is set.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Cycle Counting

sentences.text: If CCV=0, the CC value might not hold the correct count of elapsed active cycles since the last recorded transfer.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Cycle Counting

sentences.text: The next record will have CCV=0 after a write to `xctrctl`, or execution of SCTRCLR, since CtrCycleCounter is reset. CCV should additionally be cleared after any other implementation-specific scenarios where active cycles might not be counted in CtrCycleCounter.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Cycle Counting

sentences.text: When the optional `xctrctl`.RASEMU bit is implemented and set to 1, transfer recording behavior is altered to emulate the behavior of a return-address stack (RAS).
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > RAS (Return Address Stack) Emulation Mode

sentences.text: Indirect and direct calls are recorded as normal * Function returns pop the most recent call, by decrementing the WRPTR then invalidating the WRPTR entry (by setting ctrsource.V=0).
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > RAS (Return Address Stack) Emulation Mode

sentences.text: As a result, logical entry 0 is invalidated and moves to logical entry depth-1, while logical entries 1..depth-1 move to 0..depth-2. * Co-routine swaps affect both a return and a call.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > RAS (Return Address Stack) Emulation Mode

sentences.text: Logical entry 0 is overwritten, and WRPTR is not modified. * Other transfer types are inhibited * Transfer type filtering bits (`xctrctl`[47:32]) and external trap enable bits (`xctrctl`.xTE) are ignored
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > RAS (Return Address Stack) Emulation Mode

sentences.text: [NOTE] Profiling tools often collect call stacks along with each sample.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > RAS (Return Address Stack) Emulation Mode

sentences.text: Stack walking, however, is a complex and often slow process that may require recompilation (e.g., -fno-omit-frame-pointer) to work reliably.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > RAS (Return Address Stack) Emulation Mode

sentences.text: With RAS emulation, tools can ask CTR hardware to save call stacks even for unmodified code.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > RAS (Return Address Stack) Emulation Mode

sentences.text: CTR RAS emulation has limitations.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > RAS (Return Address Stack) Emulation Mode

sentences.text: The CTR buffer will contain only partial stacks in cases where the call stack depth was greater than the CTR depth, CTR recording was enabled at a lower point in the call stack than main(), or where the CTR buffer was cleared since main().
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > RAS (Return Address Stack) Emulation Mode

sentences.text: The CTR stack may be corrupted in cases where calls and returns are not symmetric, such as with stack unwinding (e.g., setjmp/longjmp, C++ exceptions), where stale call entries may be left on the CTR stack, or user stack switching, where calls from multiple stacks may be intermixed.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > RAS (Return Address Stack) Emulation Mode

sentences.text: [NOTE] As described in , when CCV=1, the CC field provides the elapsed cycles since the prior CTR entry was recorded.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > RAS (Return Address Stack) Emulation Mode

sentences.text: This introduces implementation challenges when RASEMU=1 because, for each recorded call, there may have been several recorded calls (and returns which “popped” them) since the prior remaining call entry was recorded (see ).
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > RAS (Return Address Stack) Emulation Mode

sentences.text: The implication is that returns that pop a call entry not only do not reset the cycle counter, but instead add the CC field from the popped entry to the counter.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > RAS (Return Address Stack) Emulation Mode

sentences.text: For simplicity, an implementation may opt to record CCV=0 for all calls, or those whose parent call was popped, when RASEMU=1.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > RAS (Return Address Stack) Emulation Mode

sentences.text: When `sctrstatus`.FROZEN=1, transfer recording is inhibited.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Freeze

sentences.text: This bit can be set by hardware, as described below, or by software.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Freeze

sentences.text: When `sctrctl`.LCOFIFRZ=1 and a local-counter-overflow interrupt (LCOFI) traps (as a result of an HPM counter overflow) to M-mode or to S-mode, `sctrstatus`.FROZEN is set by hardware.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Freeze

sentences.text: This inhibits CTR recording until software clears FROZEN.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Freeze

sentences.text: The LCOFI trap itself is not recorded. [NOTE] Freeze on LCOFI ensures that the execution path leading to the sampled instruction (`xepc`) is preserved, and that the local-counter-overflow interrupt (LCOFI) and associated Interrupt Service Routine (ISR) do not displace any recorded transfer history state.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Freeze

sentences.text: It is the responsibility of the ISR to clear FROZEN before xRET, if continued control transfer recording is desired.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Freeze

sentences.text: LCOFI refers only to architectural traps directly caused by a local counter overflow.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Freeze

sentences.text: If a local-counter-overflow interrupt is recognized without a trap, FROZEN is not automatically set.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Freeze

sentences.text: For instance, no freeze occurs if the LCOFI is pended while interrupts are masked, and software recognizes the LCOFI (perhaps by reading `stopi` or `sip`) and clears `sip`.LCOFIP before the trap is raised.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Freeze

sentences.text: As a result, some or all CTR history may be overwritten while handling the LCOFI.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Freeze

sentences.text: Such cases are expected to be very rare; for most usages (e.g., application profiling) privilege mode filtering is sufficient to ensure that CTR updates are inhibited while interrupts are handled in a more privileged mode.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Freeze

sentences.text: Similarly, on a breakpoint exception that traps to M-mode or S-mode with `sctrctl`.BPFRZ=1, FROZEN is set by hardware.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Freeze

sentences.text: The breakpoint exception itself is not recorded.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Freeze

sentences.text: [NOTE] Breakpoint exception refers to synchronous exceptions with a cause value of Breakpoint (3), regardless of source (ebreak, c.ebreak, Sdtrig); it does not include entry into Debug Mode, even in cores where this is implemented as an exception.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Freeze

sentences.text: If the H extension is implemented, freeze behavior for LCOFIs and breakpoint exceptions that trap to VS-mode is determined by the LCOFIFRZ and BPFRZ values, respectively, in `vsctrctl`.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Freeze

sentences.text: This includes virtual LCOFIs pended by a hypervisor.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Freeze

sentences.text: When a guest uses the SBI Supervisor Software Events (SSE) extension, the LCOFI will trap to HS-mode, which will then invoke a registered VS-mode LCOFI handler routine.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Freeze

sentences.text: If `vsctrctl`.LCOFIFRZ=1, the HS-mode handler will need to emulate the freeze by setting `sctrstatus`.FROZEN=1 before invoking the registered handler routine.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Behavior > Freeze

sentences.text: Any custom CTR extension must be associated with a non-zero value within the designated custom bits in `xctrctl`.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Custom Extensions

sentences.text: When the custom bits hold a non-zero value that enables a custom extension, the extension may alter standard CTR behavior, and may define new custom status fields within `sctrstatus` or the CTR .
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Custom Extensions

sentences.text: All custom status fields, and standard status fields whose behavior is altered by the custom extension, must revert to standard behavior when the custom bits hold zero.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Custom Extensions

sentences.text: This includes read-only 0 behavior for any bits undefined by any implemented standard extensions.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smctr" Control Transfer Records Extension, Version 1.0 > CSRs > Custom Extensions
